title: Vulpia teneriffae
intro: Vulpia teneriffae, also known as Canary Island Fescue Grass, is a perennial grass species native to the Canary Islands. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects, making it beneficial for treating various health issues, such as wounds, fever, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Vulpia teneriffae include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Vulpia teneriffae is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat coughs, sore throats, and digestive issues, and as a natural remedy for colds and flu.
taxonomy: Vulpia teneriffae is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It is native to the Canary Islands, specifically Tenerife. The species is also known as Teneriffe fescue or Canary fescue. Vulpia teneriffae belongs to the tribe Poeae and is often classified as a subspecies of the species Vulpia bromoides. Within the genus Vulpia, Vulpia teneriffae is a distinct species.
uses: Vulpia teneriffae, also known as Canary Island fescue, is a plant species native to the Canary Islands. Vulpia teneriffae has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory properties.
